attributes.description: Alerts when the average runnable goroutines per CPU on a node exceeds 30 (`sys_runnable_goroutines_per_cpu > 30`). This is CockroachDB's canonical indicator that the Go scheduler cannot keep up and the process is CPU-bound.
attributes.name: [CockroachDB OTel] Runnable goroutines per CPU high

investigation_guide.blob:
## CockroachDB runnable goroutines per CPU high

### What fired
`sys_runnable_goroutines_per_cpu` exceeded 30 on at least one node over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
This metric is CockroachDB's preferred indicator of CPU saturation inside the process — it reflects scheduling pressure regardless of what the host CPU gauge shows. Sustained values above 30 produce elevated tail latency across all workloads.

### Triage
1. Cross-check with `sys_cpu_combined_percent_normalized` and `sys_cpu_host_combined_percent_normalized` — both should be high if the node is truly CPU-bound.
2. Look at the workload: `sql_statements_active`, `sql_distsql_contended_queries_count`, `sql_full_scan_count` rate.
3. Inspect leaseholder/replica balance (`replicas_leaseholders`, `rebalancing_queriespersecond`) — one node holding too many leases produces this pattern.
4. Check for GC or compaction storms (`rocksdb_compactions` rate) that can saturate CPU indirectly.

### Remediation
- Rebalance leases / add nodes.
- Eliminate expensive queries (full scans, large joins) surfaced by the application.
- Upgrade instance size if the workload is simply beyond provisioned cores.

### Tuning
- `> 30` is the documented overload threshold. Lower only if you need earlier warning.
- Default 10-minute window filters brief GC/scheduling spikes.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-cockroachdb.otel-default*
| WHERE service.name == "cockroachdb"
  AND sys_runnable_goroutines_per_cpu IS NOT NULL
  AND sys_runnable_goroutines_per_cpu > 30
// Values > 30 indicate CPU overload
| WHERE attributes.node_id IS NOT NULL AND attributes.store IS NULL
| STATS goroutines_per_cpu = MAX(sys_runnable_goroutines_per_cpu) BY attributes.node_id, host.name
| SORT goroutines_per_cpu DESC
